mnemonics: C-LINK: Contiguous data Linked by pointers, Next node's address kept.
keyPoints: A chain is represented by a linked list where each node contains data and a pointer to the next node.
The chain starts with a 'head' pointer that holds the address of the first node.
The end of the chain is marked by a node whose pointer is NULL.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to set the 'next' pointer of the last node to NULL, causing undefined behavior when traversing.
Losing the head pointer by accidentally overwriting it, making the entire chain inaccessible and causing a memory leak.
